question:
Directions: Study the following information

carefully and answer
the questions given

below:

Twelve students are sitting in parallel rows. E, F,

G, H, I and J sit in row 1 while M, N, O, P, Q and R

sit in row 2. Students in row 1 and row 2 face

each other. Students of row 1 face south while

students of row 2 face north.

F sits second to the right of G, who faces O.

One person sits between O and Q, who sits at

one of the extreme ends. F does not sit at any

extreme end. M sits to the immediate right of N,

who sits fifth left to the Q. R sits opposite I, who

is not adjacent to F. I sits immediately right to

the H. J sits second to the right of E but not

opposite to P.

How many students sit between M and Q in the

same row?

question:
Study the following data carefully and answer

the questions accordingly.

Ten people sit in a two-parallel row. Five people

in each row. In row-1 A, B, C, D and E are seated

and all face towards south. In row-2 P, Q, R, S

and T are seated and all face towards the

north. People seated in row-1 face people

seated in row-2. S sits in the middle of the row.

The immediate neighbor of S faces E. E sits

three places away from B. T sits diagonally

opposite to B. A faces P. C and Q does not sit

on any of the extreme ends of the row. D sits

right of B.

Four of the following five are alike in a certain

way based on their positions in the above

arrangement and so form a group. Which of the

following does not belong to the group ?

question:
Read the following information carefully and

answer the questions given below.

Twelve people are sitting in two parallel rows

which has seven seats in each row such that

one of the seats in each row is vacant. There is

equal distance between adjacent seats. In row

1 i.e. H, I, J, K, L and M are seated and all of

them are facing towards north. In row 2 i.e. P, Q,

R, S, T and U seated and all of them are facing

towards south. Therefore, each member in one

row face another person in other row except

one person as one person as one seat is

vacant.

Note 1: One seat in between two people

doesn’t necessarily mean that there is a person

sitting on the seat between the two people.

Note 2: One person sitting between the two

people doesn’t necessarily mean that there is

only one seat in between the two persons.

I sits on the third seat from the left end of the

row. The one who faces R sits second to the left

of J. Only two people sit between I and J. The

one who faces K sits third to the right of R. Q is

not an immediate neighbor of R. Only three

people sit between K and the one who faces Q.

Only two people sit between Q and S who

neither faces K nor I. There are two persons

between P and U. P sits to the right of U. P

doesn’t sit at the extreme end of the row. The

one who sits to the immediate left of S faces

the one who sits to the immediate left of H who

is not an immediate neighbour of J. M sits

second to the left of L.

Four of the following five are alike in a certain

way and hence form a group. Which of the

following does not belong to that group?
